meaning: adj.鍗冧竾浜垮垎涔嬩竴鐨刟dv.鍗冧竾浜垮垎涔嬩竴鍦皀.鍗冧竾浜垮垎涔嬩竴
examples: That precise characterization allowed them to target molecules with laser pulses lasting for one-quadrillionth of a second, or just long enough to set single electrons spinning.
A femtosecond laser pulse lasts on the order of a quadrillionth of a second but reaches a peak power equivalent to that of the entire power grid of North America during its short burst.
The technology uses a femtosecond laser, which means it pulses at speeds of one-quadrillionth of a second.
The sensor's imaging chip picks up the dark string immediately, even if only 1 femtogram (one-quadrillionth of a gram) of TNT is stuck to it.
